result:
All four are real (inline) functions, not macros. Here's the full list in source order:

**`ksys_read()`** — `fs/read_write.c:602-619`

| Line | Call | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 604 | `fdget_pos(fd)` | `static inline struct fd fdget_pos(int fd)` in `include/linux/file.h:72` — inline function, not a macro. Internally calls `__to_fd(__fdget_pos(fd))`, where `__fdget_pos` is the real (extern, defined in `fs/file.c:1057`) resolver. |
| 608 | `file_ppos(f.file)` | `static inline loff_t *file_ppos(struct file *file)` defined locally just above at `fs/read_write.c:597-600`. Not a macro. |
| 613 | `vfs_read(f.file, buf, count, ppos)` | Ordinary extern function, defined in the same file at `fs/read_write.c:450`. |
| 616 | `fdput_pos(f)` | `static inline void fdput_pos(struct fd f)` in `include/linux/file.h:77` — inline function, not a macro. Internally calls `fdput(f)` (and conditionally `__f_unlock_pos`). |

No genuine preprocessor macros are invoked as "calls" in the function body — all four call sites resolve to `static inline` functions or plain extern functions, none are `#define` macros.
